data.reasoningText:
The user wants to enable hot reload (dotnet watch) for their test project without setting an environment variable every time. For `dotnet test`, hot reload is typically done via `dotnet watch test`. But they mention not wanting to set an environment variable each time.

Actually, for `dotnet test` with the newer testing platform (`TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport`), there's a `DOTNET_WATCH` or `TESTINGPLATFORM_HOTRELOAD_ENABLED` environment variable. But the user wants to avoid setting env vars.

Actually, I think the user is referring to `DOTNET_WATCH_SUPPRESS_LAUNCH_BROWSER` or more likely `DOTNET_WATCH` related settings. 

With .NET 9 and the Microsoft Testing Platform, I can enable hot reload for tests either through the `TESTINGPLATFORM_HOTRELOAD_ENABLED` environment variable or by using the `--hotreload` flag directly with `dotnet test`.

Since the user doesn't want to set environment variables repeatedly, the cleanest solution is to configure the `TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments` MSBuild property in the project file to automatically pass `--hotreload` whenever tests run.

Setting this property in the csproj...

data.content: I added `<TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--hotreload</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>` to your csproj. Since your project uses the Microsoft Testing Platform (`TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport`), this property automatically passes `--hotreload` whenever you run `dotnet test` — no environment variable needed.
